author: govinda dāsa
verses:
śrī-kṛṣṇa-caitanya, balarāma nityānanda,
pāriṣada⋅saṅge avatāra
golokera prema-dhana, sabāre jāciñā dila,
nā loinu muñi durācāra

āre pāmara mana, marame rahala baḍa śela
saṅkīrtana⋅prema⋅bādale, saba hiyā ḍubala,
mohe vidhi vañcita kaila

śrī⋅guru⋅vaiṣṇavapada, kalpataru⋅chāyā pāñā,
saba jīva tāpa pāsarila
muñi abhāgiyā viṣa⋅, viṣaye mātiyā roinu,
hena juge nistāra nā haila

āgune puḍiyā maro, jale paraveśa karo,
viṣa khāñā maro mo pāpiyā
ei mata kari’ jadi, maraṇa nā kare vidhi,
prāṇa rahe ki sukha lāgiyā

e hena gaurāṅga⋅guṇa, nā karinu śravaṇa,
hāya hāya, kari hā hutāśa
‘hare kṛṣṇa’⋅mahāmantra, mukha bhari’ nā loilāma,
jīvanmṛta govindadāsa
en_translation:
Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya and Nityānanda, along with Their associates, have descended to this world. They distributed the treasure of divine love from Goloka to everyone, but I, a wicked soul, did not accept it.

O my wretched mind! A great spear remains in my heart. In the shower of saṅkīrtana and divine love, all hearts were immersed, but destiny has cheated me through illusion.

Having attained the shade of the wish-fulfilling tree of the feet of the Guru and the Vaiṣṇavas, all living entities forgot their miseries. I, an unfortunate soul, remained absorbed in poisonous sense objects, and thus I did not attain deliverance in this age.

I would rather die by fire, drown in water, or die by eating poison. If destiny does not grant me death in this way, for what happiness does my life still remain?

I did not hear the glorious qualities of such a Lord Gaurāṅga. Alas, I cry out in lamentation! I did not chant the Hare Kṛṣṇa Mahā-mantra to my heart's content. I am but the living dead, says Govinda Dāsa.
